title: Antoinette Brown Blackwell: Voting for Change
description: A historical coloring page showing Antoinette Brown Blackwell voting, highlighting her role as a women's rights advocate who fought for women's suffrage in the 1800s.
instructions: Color this picture of Antoinette Brown Blackwell casting her vote. Use your favorite colors and try to stay inside the lines. As you color, think about how brave she was to stand up for women's right to vote!

content.image: Antoinette Brown Blackwell standing proudly at a voting booth. She is wearing a long Victorian-style dress with a high collar and her hair is pulled back. She is placing her ballot into a ballot box labeled 'VOTES'. There are other people in the background watching this historic moment. She has a determined, happy expression on her face.

learningFacts: Antoinette Brown Blackwell fought for women's right to vote in the 1800s
Women weren't allowed to vote in America until 1920
Antoinette was also America's first woman minister
She believed girls and women should have the same rights as boys and men
Brave people like Antoinette helped change unfair rules
